resolution: Add the missing package to your requirements.txt or rebuild the Docker image.
Check the IAM roles for the service account and ensure it has access to both BigQuery and the underlying source (e.g., Google Drive).
Align the column names and types between the query and the target table.
Explicitly define the schema instead of relying on automatic inference.
Check network latency or increase the timeout setting in the operator.
Verify the path or table name and ensure it was created in previous steps.
Increase the memory limit of the container or optimize the code to handle data in smaller chunks.
Implement exponential backoff or request a quota increase from the cloud provider console.
Check if the DB server is running and verify firewall/security group rules for the worker IP.
Clean up the /tmp directory or increase the disk size of the worker/PVC.
Check the SQL string for missing commas, incorrect keywords, or unclosed quotes.
Update the CA certificates in the environment or check if the target's certificate is expired.
Check executor memory settings or increase the number of retries for Spark tasks.
Inspect the source data for unexpected NULL values or update the target table schema.
Refresh the service account token or update the connection credentials in Airflow.
The task should be retried automatically. If it happens frequently, check pod priority settings.
Reduce parallelism for this task or check for long-running transactions locking the same tables.
Define the missing environment variable in the Airflow deployment or Dockerfile.
Verify the source file integrity and check for unexpected delimiters or header changes.
Check if multiple processes are trying to write to the same GCS object simultaneously.
